rationale: Answer key marks this as a CONTROL case (matched control for T17 MRVL; SIC 3674, no scheme). dim1: output gives misstatement_probability=25, which for a control case falls in the p<=30 band → 2. dim2: control case → null per rubric; note the output correctly provides an empty mechanism_hypotheses list, consistent with p=25 (no hypothesis required below 40). dim3: control case → nulls per rubric. dim4: consistency rule check — p=25 (<40) requires clear/watch; declared risk_tier='watch', so no cap. The evidence is specific and grounded in provided data: CL1 pairs exact revenue and AR values with accession numbers (e.g., SalesRevenueNet=2382531000 FY2014 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=267833000, computing AR +16.9% vs revenue +9.9%); CL3 contrasts NetIncomeLoss=648216000 with operating cash flow 790780000 for FY2015; CL6 tracks the doubtful-accounts allowance from 3446000 (2012-03-31) to 3353000 (2015-03-28) against AR growth 214965000→246615000. The top_signals section coherently combines multi-year trends (inventory doubling FY2010→FY2011 vs 29% revenue growth; gross margin trajectory 63.4%→70.2% over 5 years; consecutive-year AR/revenue divergence) and appropriately weighs offsetting positives (OCF > NI every year, clean filing chronology). This is a coherent multi-year, multi-point combination genuinely supporting the low-probability conclusion → 3. memorization_suspect_condition2: false — the reasoning repeatedly cites concrete provided data content (specific XBRL values with dates and accession numbers), not mere document lists, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no reference to enforcement, revelation, or outcomes; the company is treated purely on its financials).
